text:
Market today: Geopolitical tensions are flaring with US strikes in the Middle East and threats to shipping. This creates a risk-off mood, which typically hits growth-oriented tech and chip names hard due to potential supply chain disruptions and broader market uncertainty.

Your holdings: SOXL (-46.24%) and SPAL (-51.33%) are your biggest concerns. Both are leveraged ETFs, meaning drawdowns are amplified, and their listed stops are significantly above current prices, suggesting they've been blown. SPAL is a brand-new, very volatile IPO. QQQ (-2.6%), SMH (-12.19%), and QQQI (-3.79%) are also in "SELL" status and negative. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on the Middle East situation for any escalation or de-escalation, as this will heavily influence overall market sentiment. Also, Alphabet's earnings could provide insights into big tech's spending and profitability in data centers.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management on your leveraged positions and stay defensive given the geopolitical backdrop.
plays: Review SOXL: Your stop at 200 is far above the current price of 136.81. Re-evaluate this leveraged position given the significant -46.24% loss and its decay characteristic.
Review SPAL: Similar to SOXL, its stop at 21.38 is above the current 12.24. Address this brand-new, volatile, leveraged ETF with a -51.33% loss immediately.
No new buys today - hold and watch. Geopolitical risks and significant losses in core holdings warrant caution over new positions.
